title: [lichen_l2.c:204-208] rx_ipv6_buf not protected during net_pkt_write after mutex release
description:
Gap found: RX buffer protection timing not analyzed.

Why this was missed: Mutex scope in lichen_l2_input reviewed but not buffer usage after release.

The actual bug: In lichen_l2_input(), rx_ipv6_buf is filled under rx_mutex (lines 1163-1166), then the mutex is released at line 1223, and THEN net_pkt_write() is called at line 1215. Wait - looking again, the mutex is released at 1223 which is AFTER net_pkt_write at 1215. The actual sequence:

1. Lock rx_mutex (1101)
2. Fill rx_ipv6_buf (1163-1166)
3. Allocate net_pkt (1205-1208)
4. net_pkt_write from rx_ipv6_buf (1215)
5. Unlock rx_mutex (1223)
6. net_recv_data (1234)

This is correct - buffer is protected during use. However, the allocation at 1205 can block for CONFIG_LICHEN_L2_RX_ALLOC_TIMEOUT_MS while holding the mutex, preventing concurrent RX processing. This was partially noted in Round 1 (project-LICHEN-tvfm.38) but the blocking-while-holding-mutex aspect wasn't explicit. Priority 4 - design choice, not bug.
close_reason: Documented: net_pkt_write() copies data from rx_ipv6_buf while mutex is held; after unlock net_recv_data() uses pkt (independent copy). Not a real concern.
